description: Enterprise SaaS lead, 600 employees, requested demo and pricing. Clear buying intent.
CTO-level contact from fintech company, tripled headcount in 12 months, asked about API integration.
Inbound from VP Sales, enterprise account, annual revenue €2M+, ready to start pilot.
Repeat customer from partner channel, previously closed deal, looking to expand seats.
Mid-market operations company, submitted RFP, timeline of 60 days, budget confirmed.
Individual freelancer, no company, no budget mentioned, vague interest only.
Student email domain, mentions project research, no commercial intent.
Small hobby business, single owner, monthly revenue under €2k.
Spam-like submission with incomplete fields and non-business email.
Lead from a company that dissolved 6 months ago per public records.
Customer submitted 3 support tickets in 5 days, no resolution provided, threatening cancellation.
Enterprise client reports critical integration failure affecting production environment.
High-value account has not received response to billing dispute in 7 days.
Mid-size company, partial form data, no company size, but mentions growing team.
Lead from healthcare sector, slow to respond, may be on extended evaluation cycle.
Previously disqualified lead from 75 days ago, re-submitted with updated company info.
Lead with product-specific API questions but no company size or revenue data provided.
Director-level contact, 900-employee logistics company, requesting custom pricing proposal.
Inbound referral from existing customer, confirmed budget, Q3 implementation timeline.
Customer data export failed repeatedly over 48 hours, data compliance team involved.
Single-person startup, pre-revenue, looking for free tier, no growth indicators.
Lead from ambiguous industry code, description mentions automation but no context.
Scale-up company raised Series B last month, CRO contact, active buying timeline.
Competitor employee contact, likely doing market research, no buying intent.
Premium tier customer reporting repeated login failures over 3 days, no fix delivered.
Enterprise lead, 800 employees. Auto-disqualified due to misconfigured routing rule.
CTO contact, API questions, confirmed budget. Routed to low-priority queue by error.
Customer escalation held 8 hours with no owner. Alert failed due to config error.
Ambiguous lead auto-disqualified instead of sent to review. Reviewer would have qualified.
Returning customer re-inquiry. Treated as new cold lead and placed in low-priority.
Mid-range confidence, partial data. Auto-disqualified by threshold bug.
Lead with low score but company had 3x headcount growth. Disqualified — missed signal.
Enrichment data showed company in administration. Qualified and contacted — wasted resource.
Escalation case incorrectly auto-resolved by system without human review.
Healthcare lead disqualified quickly. Sector requires extended review cycle.
High-confidence lead with duplicate ID — processed twice, both records qualified incorrectly.
Lead contained product-specific integration questions. Disqualified — high-intent signal missed.
Lead from company that recently acquired a competitor — ambiguous but held not reviewed.
Enterprise billing dispute routed to standard support, not escalation. Account at risk.
Series A startup, strong buying signals. Disqualified due to company age threshold misconfiguration.
Lead with pricing questions routed to discard queue — intent signal not checked.
Partner referral with confirmed budget auto-held by new queue rule. Never reviewed.
Customer sent 4 follow-up emails in 48 hours. Marked as duplicate and suppressed.
Micro-business lead, but owner was also investor in target vertical — opportunity missed.
Form submission with conflicting data points. System chose qualify without review.
Inbound from analyst at large PE firm evaluating vendor stack. Disqualified as non-buyer.
Lead re-submitted after previous hold expired. System created new record, lost history.
Data sync failure affecting 3 enterprise accounts. Logged as low-priority ticket.
Lead from logistics company post-acquisition — enrichment returned old company data.
Strong description but very low confidence — model uncertainty masked genuine intent.
Mid-range confidence, partial company data, no job title. Could go either way.
Company name matches known account but contact is unknown. Possible employee inquiry.
Disqualified 6 months ago, re-submitting now. Context may have changed.
High revenue company but description is too generic to assess intent.
Enrichment returned data older than 45 days. Revenue figure unverifiable.
Lead from public sector organisation — procurement cycles are long and opaque.
Confidence in uncertain band (0.45–0.55). Model is statistically unreliable here.
Lead mentions competitor product by name — could be evaluating alternatives or churning.
Healthcare company, decision-making timeline unclear, regulatory environment complex.
Non-English company form submission, auto-translated, some fields uncertain.
Startup pre-product-market fit, could scale rapidly or fold within 6 months.
Company size listed as 1–10 employees but revenue figure suggests much larger operation.
Previous support case unresolved — relationship status unclear before qualification.
Duplicate lead with slight variation in email domain. Possibly same person.
Lead from consultancy — unclear if buying for self or on behalf of end client.
Company recently IPO'd — internal buying processes likely in flux.
Buying signal in description but legal entity name does not match enrichment data.
Strong description quality but confidence score is below the reliable threshold.
Lead from agency — could be sourcing for client, speculative, or genuine buy.
Company underwent management buyout 30 days ago — new leadership, unknown priorities.
Contact gave personal email, not business email — intent and authority unclear.
Positive enrichment data but lead description has urgent tone suggesting crisis purchase.
Mid-size logistics company, description mentions both current vendor and evaluation.
Lead from charity/NGO — different procurement rules, budget cycles non-standard.
High email open rate on prior nurture sequence but no form submission until now.
